evaluation/measure:
Performance Metrics:
* Accuracy: 82.73%–95.33% (for machine learning models interpreting EEG data and predicting various states of pain).
* Not enough information is available for other models.
evaluation/comparison:
**Comparison Performed**: No

The paper does not provide any information on the comparison of the methods discussed to publicly available tools or simpler baselines. The text primarily discusses the potential applications of AI and machine learning in treating chronic back pain but does not detail any specific benchmarking or comparison studies.
evaluation/confidence:
* Statistical Confidence: [CIs: No | p-values: Yes - 0.05]
  - The text mentions that "patients who used the AI-driven application for just 2 months experienced a statistically significant reduction in pain when compared to the control group who were just part of a rehabilitation group". This implies a p-value threshold of 0.05 for statistical significance.
